hadiths.ar:
122  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّسْي َّاك ُّ مَّز َّة ً ن ِي َّذِةِ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّامَّ إ ِلَّى صَّلاَّة ِ 
إَّلل َّي ْلِ وَّ هُّوَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى إَّلسِّوَّإك ِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It is reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} stopped using siwak 
two years before he passed away, as his teeth had weakened.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 678 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 25 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 127 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 121
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢١

hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} bout a man 
who uses his hand to clean his teeth when he gets up for the night prayer (Salat Al Layl), 
even though he has access to siwak. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If he fears that dawn is approaching, there is no harm in doing so."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Masa’il Ali ibn Ja’far, Part 1, p. 250 • Qurb al-Isnad, Part 
1, p. 207 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 50 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 127 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 341 
• Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 207 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 122
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٢

hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} said: "If it were not burdensome for my community, I would 
have commanded them to use siwak (tooth cleaning) with each wudu (ablution) before 
every prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 561 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 293 • 
Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 72 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 50 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 2, p. 21 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 17 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 19 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 126 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, 
p. 137 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 123
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٣

hadiths.ar:
125 - وَّ رُّوِى هُّ ي َّعَّالَّى إ ِلَّي ْهَّا 
ي َّا كَّعْن َّه ُّ ق َّا ِن ِّى  مُّن ْذِلُّك ِ ن ِهِمْ ق َّوْماً ي َّي َّن َّط  َّق ُّون َّ ن ِق ُّص ْن َّان ِ إَّلس  َّح َّزِ ث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ن َّن ِي  َّهُّ مُّحَّم َّذإً صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ 
إ لِهِ ن َّز َّلَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلز ُّوحُّ إَّلْأ َّمِي ن ُّ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي لُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن ِالسِّوَّإك ِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "If people knew what benefits siwak holds, they would keep it 
with them in their bedding at night." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 18 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 6, p. 677 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 10 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 12 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 130 
• Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 342 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 124
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٤

hadiths.ar:
126  - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّ ي ُّزْص ِى  إَّلز َّجْمَّن َّ وَّ ي ُّن َّي ِّض ُّ إَّلْأ َّسْن َّان َّ وَّ ي َّذ ْهَّث ُّ ن ِالْحَّق ْزِ وَّ ي َّس ُّذ ُّ إَّللِّي َّه َّ وَّ ي ُّس َّهِّى  إَّلط َّعَّامَّ وَّ ي ذ ْهَّث ُّ ن ِالْن َّلْ ع َّمِ وَّ ي َّز ِي ذُّ ق ِى   
إَّلْحِق ْظ ِ وَّ ي ُّص َّاعِف ُّ إَّلْحَّسَّن َّاب ِ وَّ ي َّق ْزَّحُّ ن ِهِ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ُّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "The Kaaba complained to Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, about 
the distress it suffered from the breaths of the polytheists.  
Allah {SWT}, Exalted be He, revealed to it: 'Calm yourself, O’ Kaaba, for I will replace them 
with a people who purify themselves with branches of trees.'  
When Allah {SWT} sent His Prophet Muhammad {saws}, the Trustworthy Spirit, Archangel 
Jibril {a.s}, descended upon him with the siwak (toothstick)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 50 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 677 
• Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 23 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 137  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 125
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٥

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Siwak (tooth cleaning) has twelve benefits: it is part of the Sunnah (tradition), purifies 
the mouth, improves vision, pleases the Merciful, whitens the teeth, removes decay, 
strengthens the gums, increases appetite, removes phlegm, enhances memory, 
multiplies good deeds, and brings joy to the angels." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 562 • Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿIqāb al-
Aʿmāl, Part 1, p. 17 • Al -Khasal, Part 2, p. 481 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 50 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, 
p. 320 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 129 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 346

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 126
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٦

hadiths.ar:
128  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِلَّى مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق ِي مَّا كَّي َّث َّ مِن ْ   ج َّوَّإب ِ مَّسَّان ِلِهِ: 
«إَّلْوَّج ْهِ وَّ إَّلذ ِّرَّإعَّي ْن ِ وَّ مَّسْحُّ إَّلز َّإ ْسِ وَّ إَّلْق َّذَّمَّي ْن ِ ق َّلِق ِي َّامِهِ ن َّي ْن 
ي َّذَّى ِ إَّلل َّهِ ي َّعَّالَّى وَّ إِسْي ِق ْن َّالِهِ إ ِي  َّاةُّ ن ِح َّوَّإرِجِهِ إَّلط  َّاهِزَّة ِ وَّ مُّلاَّق َّاي ِهِ ن ِهَّا إَّلْكِزَّإمَّ إَّلْكَّاي ِن ِ ي ن َّ ق َّي َّع ْسِلُّ إَّلْوَّج ْهَّ لِلس ُّح ُّوذِ وَّ  
إَّلْح ُّص ُّوعِ وَّ ي َّع ْسِلُّ إَّلْي َّذَّي ْن ِ لِي ُّق َّلِّن َّهُّمَّا وَّ ي َّزْع َّث َّ ن ِهِمَّا وَّ ي َّزْهَّث َّ وَّ ي َّي َّن َّي  َّلَّ وَّ ي َّمْسَّحُّ إَّلز َّإ ْسَّ وَّ  إَّلْق َّذَّمَّي ْن ِ لِأ َّن  َّهُّمَّا ط َّاهِزَّإن ِ 
مَّكْس ُّوق َّان ِ ي َّسْي َّق ْن ِلُّ ن ِهِمَّا كُّل َّ جَّالأَّي ِهِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ ق ِي هِمَّا مِن َّ إَّلْح ُّص ُّوعِ وَّ  إَّلي  َّن َّي  ُّلِ مَّا ق ِى  إَّلْوَّج ْهِ وَّ إَّلذ ِّرَّإعَّي ْن ِ .
hadiths.en:
A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah {saws} and asked Him about 
various matters. Among the questions they asked was: ‘Tell us, O’ Muhammad, why 
these four parts are washed in ablution when they are the cleanest parts of the body?'  
The Prophet {saws} replied: "When Satan whispered to Adam {a.s}, he approached the tree 
and looked at it, and thus he lost the brightness of his face. Then he walked towards it 
- this was the first step taken towards wrongdoing. 
He reached out with his hand to take from it, and he ate. Consequently, the ornaments 
and coverings left his body, and he placed his hands on his head and wept.  
When Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, accepted his repentance, He made it obligatory 
upon him and his descendants to purify these four parts.  
Thus, Allah {SWT} commanded the washing of the face because he had looked at the 
tree, the washing of the hands up to the elbows because he had used them to take 
from it, the wiping of the head because he had placed his hand on his head, and the 
wiping of the feet because he had walked with them towards the wrongdoing 
(khatee'ah)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 280 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 368 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 395 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 596 • Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa’iq, Part 4, p. 52 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 12, H. 127
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢، ح ١٢٧

hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ar -Ridha {a.s} wrote to Muhammad ibn Sinan in response 
to his questions: "The reason for wudu (ablution), for which it has become obligatory 
for a servant to wash the face and arms, and to wipe the head and feet, is to prepare 
to stand before Allah {SWT} Almighty, to face Him with one’s visible limbs, and to 
encounter the noble recording angels with them.  
The face is washed for the act of prostration and submission. The hands are washed 
so that one may turn them over in prayer, supplicating with them, expressing hope and 
reverence, and focusing in worship. The head and feet are wiped because they are 
exposed, facing all conditions, and do not possess the same level of humility and 
dedication as the face and arms." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 280 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 369 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 395 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 231

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 12, H. 128
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢، ح ١٢٨

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
130 - ورُّوِىإَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّالَّ  
لأَّ إ ُّطِي ق ُّهَّا ق َّلَّمْ ي َّز َّإلُّوإ ن ِهِ جَّي  َّى رَّذ ُّوةُّ إ ِلَّى وَّإجِذَّة ٍ ق َّق َّالَّ لأَّ إ ُّطِي ق ُّهَّا ق َّق َّالُّوإ   لأَّ ن ُّذ َّ مِن ْهَّا ق َّالَّ ق َّن ِمَّا ي َّح ْلِذُّون ِي هَّا ق َّالُّوإ ن َّح ْلِذُّك َّ  
ن ِا َّن  َّك َّ صَّل َّي ْث َّ ي َّوْماً ن ِع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ وَّ مَّزَّرْب َّ عَّلَّى ص َّعِي ف ٍ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّن ْصُّزْةُّ ق َّح َّلَّذُّوةُّ ج َّلْذَّة ً مِن ْ عَّذ َّإب ِ إَّ لل َّهِ ي َّعَّالَّى ق َّامْي َّلَّا َّ ق َّن ْزُّةُّ 
ن َّارإً .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} said: "There is no prayer without 
purification”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Da‘ā’im al-Islām, Part 1, p. 100 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 
2, p. 140 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 2, p. 209 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 8 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 365 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 7, p. 43 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 365 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 366 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 368 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 129
reference.chapter: ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.location: Volume 1 › ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤، ح ١٢٩

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
131  -  وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
إَّلن  َّاس ِز ُّ عَّن ْ ر َّوْج ِهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا سَّاج ِظ  وَّ مَّان ِغُّ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ وَّ إ ِمَّامُّ ق َّوْمٍ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِهِمْ وَّ هُّمْ لَّهُّ ك ارِهُّون َّ وَّ ي َّارِك ُّ إَّلْ وُّص ُّوءِ وَّ 
إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ُّ إَّلْمُّذْرِكَّه ُّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِع َّي ْزِ ج ِمَّارٍ وَّ إَّلز ِّن ِّي ن ُّ وَّ هُّوَّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّذَّإق ِغُّ إَّلْن َّوْلَّ وَّ إَّلْع َّان ِظَّ وَّ  إَّلس َّكْزَّإن ُّ .  
وي ارك  إلوص وء ن اسي ا مي ى ذ كز ق علي ه إ ن  ي ي وص ا  وي عي ذ إلصلاة .
hadiths.en:
(Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} ) narrated: 
"A certain man from among the scholars was placed in his grave, and it was said to 
him:  
'We will strike you with one hundred lashes from the punishment of Allah {SWT}, Mighty 
and Majestic.' He replied: 'I cannot bear it.'  
They continued to reduce the punishment, until it was brought down to one lash.  
(Still) He said: 'I still cannot bear it.'  
They replied: 'It is unavoidable.'  
He asked: 'Why will you lash me?'  
They said: 'We will lash you because one day you prayed without wudu (ablution), and 
because you passed by a weak person and did not help him.'  
So they lashed him once with a punishment from Allah {SWT}, and his grave was filled 
with fire." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 43 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 130
reference.chapter: ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.location: Volume 1 › ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤، ح ١٣٠

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
132  -  وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ مَّا لأَّ ي َّعْلَّمُّون َّ وَّ مَّا لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّون َّ وَّ إَّلطِّي َّزَّة ُّ وَّ إَّلْحَّسَّذُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّق َّك ُّزُّ ق ِى  إَّلْوَّسْوَّسَّه ِ ق ِى  إ لْح َّلْق ِ مَّا لَّمْ ي َّن ْطِق ِ إَّ لْأ ِن ْسَّان ُّ  
ن ِس َّق َّه ٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said:  
"Allah {SWT} does not accept the prayer of eight types of people:  
The runaway servant until he returns to his master,  
The rebellious wife whose husband is displeased with her,  
The one who withholds zakat,  
The Imam whom people dislike to pray behind,  
The one who neglects wudu,  
The mature (of age) woman who prays without a head covering,  
The one who prays while suppressing the urge to urinate or defecate (Zabeen),  
And the one who is intoxicated." 
[AL SADUQ] 
If someone forgets to perform wudu and remembers later, they must perform wudu 
and repeat the prayer. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 44 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 8, p. 348 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 131
reference.chapter: ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
133  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّن ْق َّى مِن ْ وَّج ْهِهِ إ ِذ َّإ ي َّوَّص  َّا َّ مَّوْص ِغ  لَّمْ ي ُّصِن ْهُّ  
إَّلْمَّاءُّ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} said:  
"Nine things have been lifted from my community: mistakes, errors, forgetfulness, 
what they are compelled to do, what they do not know, what they cannot endure, 
superstitions, envy (if not acted upon), and thoughts of doubt in creation as long as a 
person does not express them verbally."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 5, p. 1085 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 7, p. 293 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 8, p. 249 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 1, p. 637 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 132

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
134 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
ي َّكُّن ْ ن َّق ِى َّ ق ِى  ي َّذِك َّ مِن ْ ن َّذَّإوَّة ِ وَّص ُّون ِك َّ س َّى ْء  ق َّح ُّذ ْ مِم َّا ن َّق ِى َّ مِن ْهُّ ق ِى  لِحْي َّي ِك َّ وَّ إِمْسَّحْ ن ِهِ رَّإ ْسَّك   وَّ رِج ْلَّي ْك َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ  
ي َّكُّن ْ لَّك َّ لِحْي َّه   ق َّح ُّذ ْ مِن ْ جَّاج ِن َّي ْك َّ وَّ إ َّس ْق َّارِ عَّي ْن َّي ْك َّ وَّ إِمْسَّحْ ن ِهِ رَّإ ْسَّك َّ وَّ رِج ْلَّي ْك َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّن ْ ق َّ مِن ْ ن ِل َّه ِ وَّص ُّون ِك َّ س َّى ْء   
إ َّعَّذْب َّ إَّلْوُّص ُّوءَّ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} was asked about a man who, 
while performing wudu, misses a small area on his face that the water does not reach.  
Imam {a.s} said: "It suffices for him to moisten it with water from another part of his 
body."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 352 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 472 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 133

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
135  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّسِى َّ مَّسْحَّ رَّإ ْسِهِ ق َّال 
جَّي  َّى ذَّج َّلَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"If you forget to wipe your head, then wipe it and your feet with the remaining moisture 
from your wudu. 
If there is no moisture left in your hands from the wudu, then take what remains in your 
beard and wipe your head and feet with it.  
If you do not have a beard, then take from your eyebrows and the lashes of your eyes 
and wipe your head and feet with that.  
If there is no moisture left from your wudu, then repeat the wudu."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 351 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 409 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 134

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.ar:
136  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ر َّي ْذٍ إَّلس  َّح َّامِ وَّ إَّلْمُّق َّص  َّلِ ن ْن ِ صَّالِحٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ي َّوَّص  َّا َّ ق َّن َّسِى َّ إ َّن ْ 
ي َّمْسَّحَّ عَّلَّى رَّإ ْسِهِ جَّي  َّى ق َّامَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّال . 
ومن  س ك  ق ى  س ى  من  وص ون ه وهو ق اعذ على جال إلوص وء ق لي عذ، ومن  ق ام عن  مكان ه ي م س ك  ق ى  س ى  من  وص ون ه ق لا  
ي لي ق ث  إ لى إلس ك  إ لأ إ ن  ي سي ي ق ن ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلوص وء وهو على ي ق ي ن  من  إلحذب  ق لي ي وص ا ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلحذب  وكان  
على ي ق ي ن  من  إلوص وء ق لا ي ن ق ض  إلي ق ي ن  ن الس ك  إ لأ إ ن  ي سي ي ق ن ، ومن  كا ن  على ي ق ي ن  من  إلوص وء وإلحذب  ولأ ي ذرى  إ ي هما  
إ سن ق  ق لي ي وص ا  .
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} regarding a 
man who forgets to wipe his head during wudu.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Then let him wipe it."  
Abu Basir asked: "What if he does not remember until he has started the prayer?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Then let him wipe his head with the moisture from his beard”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 352 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 410 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 135
reference.chapter: ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.location: Volume 1 › ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤، ح ١٣٥

hadiths.chapter: 14. ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Zayd al -Shahham and al-Mufaddal ibn Salih, narrated from 
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, concerning a man who performs wudu but 
forgets to wipe his head until he has started the prayer,  
Imam {a.s} said: "Let him leave, wipe his head, and repeat the prayer." 
[AL SADUQ] 
For anyone who doubts something in their wudu while still seated in the place of wudu, 
they should redo it. However, if they have already left the place and then doubt 
something about the wudu, they should disregard the doubt unless they are certain. If 
a person is certain of a state of ritual impurity (hadath) but doubts whether they 
performed wudu, they should perform wudu. Conversely, if one is certain of wudu but 
doubts an act that would invalidate it, they should not negate their certainty due to 
doubt unless they are certain. If one is sure of both wudu and impurity but does not 
know which came first, they should perform wudu. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 352 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 371

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 14, H. 136
reference.chapter: ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.location: Volume 1 › ON THOSE WHO OMIT WUDU OR PART OF IT, OR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤، ح ١٣٦

hadiths.ar:
138 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  « 
وهذ إ إ ذ إ لم ي كن  ق ي ه ي ق ل، ق ا ذ إ كان  ق ي ه ي ق ل ق ق ي ه إلأسي ن ح اء وإلوص وء  
وكلما ج زح  من  إلطزق ي ن  من  ذم وق ي ح ومذ ى  ووذ ى  وع ي ز ذ لك  ق لا وص وء ق ي ه ولأ  إسي ن ح اء ما لم ي ح زح  ن ول إ و ع ان ظ إ و  
ري ح إ و من ى  .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah ibn A'yan asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar 
ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about what nullifies wudu (ablution).  
They said: "Anything that exits from your two lower openings, the front (urethra) and 
the back (anus), such as excrement, urine, semen, or wind, as well as sleep that 
causes a loss of consciousness, invalidates wudu”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
And the ablution is not invalidated by anything other, such as; vomiting, regurgitation, 
nosebleeds, cupping, abscesses, wounds, and sores, do not nullify ablution nor 
necessitate istinja (cleansing of private parts). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 15, H. 137
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥، ح ١٣٧

hadiths.ar:
139  -  وَّق َّال عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ َّج ِذُّ إَّلزِّي حَّ ق ِى  ن َّطْن ِى  جَّي  َّى إ َّط ُّن  َّ إ َّن  َّهَّا ق َّذْ ج َّزَّج َّث ْ 
ق َّق َّال ن َّ إ َّلْي َّي َّى ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ  
ق َّي ُّحْذِب ُّ لِي ُّس َّكِّكَّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no ablution required in the instance of taenia worms (or ticks and small 
worms), they are regarded like lice, and this applies if they do not have any waste 
matter, However, if there is waste matter, then istinja (cleansing of the private parts) 
and ablution are required.” 
[AL SADUQ] 
Anything that exits from the two lower openings (other than stool, urine, wind, or 
semen) such as blood, pus, pre -seminal fluid, or other discharges does not require 
ablution or istinja, unless it is urine, stool, wind, or semen.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 36 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 
12 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 82 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 248 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 258 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 15, H. 138
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥، ح ١٣٨

hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah asked Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'I feel wind in my stomach to the extent that I think it has exited.'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'You do not need to perform ablution unless you hear a sound or 
perceive the smell.'  
Imam {a.s} then added:  
'Indeed, Satan sits between a man's buttocks and causes him to think he has passed 
wind to make him doubt.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 246 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 15, H. 139
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥، ح ١٣٩

hadiths.ar:
141   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّان ِزٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ مِن ْ إ َّط َّاق ِي زِةِ وَّ س َّارِن ِهِ إ َّ ي َّمْسَّحُّهُّ ن ِالْمَّاءِ 
ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah asked Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} about a man who trims his nails, clips his 
mustache, and cuts some of the hair from his beard and head. Does that nullify his 
ablution?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'O’ Zurarah, all of this is Sunnah (recommended practice), while 
ablution is an obligation (fardh). None of the Sunnah practices invalidate an 
obligation. In fact, such practices increase one's purity.'.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 346 • Al-Istibsar, P 1, p. 95 
• Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 252 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 287 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 105 • Al -Fusul al -
Muhimmah, P 2, p. 17 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 15, H. 140
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥، ح ١٤٠
